source.title: Replay of rule-learning related neural patterns in the prefrontal cortex during sleep

confidence_rationale: Rat mPFC ensemble recording during post-learning slow-wave sleep. Response-selection-related PFC patterns replay preferentially in transient bouts coincident with hippocampal sharp-wave-ripple complexes. This is the most direct demonstration that rule-learning and action-relevant (not just sensory or spatial) content propagates from hippocampus to PFC via SWR-coupled replay. Confidence 0.75 because it is sleep data and the patterns are rule-shift-selection-related rather than trajectory-level action commands -- the mapping to V3 action selection is clean but one inferential step removed.
failure_signatures: Replay is measured during slow-wave sleep, not during waking internal_replay. The MECH-261 claim for waking-mode propagation to action-relevant cortex requires extension of this work into awake quiescence -- which subsequent work has begun but this paper does not test.

The PFC patterns that replay are rule-selection-related ensemble signatures, not action commands per se. Translation to action-selection bias in REE is defensible but one step beyond the direct finding.

mapping.source_claim_statement: During slow-wave sleep following rule-shift learning, rat medial prefrontal cortex ensembles replay the learning-related neural patterns in transient highly synchronised bouts. These bouts are largely coincident with hippocampal sharp-wave-ripple complexes, consistent with SWR-coupled hippocampal-to-PFC information transfer.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-261 requires that the internal_replay and offline_consolidation modes can propagate replayed content into cortical substrates that carry action-selection-relevant representations, not just sensory or spatial representations. Peyrache and colleagues demonstrate that hippocampal-SWR-coupled replay reaches medial PFC -- the cortical structure most directly implicated in action-rule representation. For V3, this supports a design in which internal_replay and offline_consolidation modes produce writes into a PFC-analog substrate (rule / meta-control level), distinct from the cortical-sensory writes demonstrated in the Rothschild 2017 entry.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The rule-shift patterns are cognitive-control ensemble signatures, not motor action commands. REE's z_policy or action-selection representation may or may not be directly analogous. The additional step from 'replay reaches rule-representing PFC' to 'replay modulates subsequent action selection' is biologically plausible but not directly tested in this paper. This paper is sleep-only; its extension to waking internal_replay requires the Carr 2011 and Tambini 2019 entries.
mapping.source_context: Multi-tetrode recording in rat mPFC during a cross-modal rule-shift task, with simultaneous hippocampal LFP. Post-learning slow-wave sleep. Explained-variance analysis (EV/REV) on PFC ensemble patterns before, during, and after learning.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (Nature Neuroscience, Battaglia / Wiener labs, rigorous explained-variance methodology). Mapping fidelity high for the rule-representation-level claim but drops for the action-selection extrapolation. Transfer risk moderate: sleep-to-waking and rule-representation-to-action-selection are both standard but not independently validated in this paper.
